# Research Task 4 — The body cost of 3,000+ wings

**Phase:** 1 (run in parallel). **Read-only. Do NOT edit the article.**
**Recommended agent:** research-analyst.

## Why this matters

The article has a strong "Pavlovian" line about Evans's body rejecting the sauce, but a fan
wonders what eating extreme capsaicin weekly for a decade actually does to him. If there's a
real, on-record quote, it's a great human beat. This task decides whether that beat ships —
it ships ONLY if backed by a genuine Evans quote or credible reporting. No speculation.

## What to find (sourced)

1. Any on-record Evans comments about physical/GI effects, tolerance, doctor visits, prep
   rituals (milk, what he does before/after), or how the wings affect him over time.
2. Any reporting on how he manages it (does he eat before, recovery, etc.).
3. Distinguish jokes from genuine statements — note which is which.

## Deliverable

A short brief (under ~200 words):

- 1–3 sourced quotes or reported facts, each with a citation URL.
- A clear verdict line: **"Ship a health beat: YES / NO"** — YES only if there's a real,
  citable quote. If it's all vague or unverifiable, say NO so the writer skips it.

## Guardrails

- This is the one "optional" addition. Default to NO if the evidence is thin. Better to skip
  than to invent a health claim.
- Don't touch the article file.
